Quaralynx Pty Ltd — Privacy & Data Handling Compliance Memo (AU), prepared for Board Risk Committee — 31 May 2026.
This note summarises APP compliance posture under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and confirms no information herein constitutes MNPI for Corporations Act/ASX purposes.
MNPI check: content is limited to policy controls and already-published privacy notices; no undisclosed financial forecasts or ASX Listing Rule 3.1 triggers.
Onboarding forms: HR templates limit fields to job-relevant data only (data minimisation), and optional marketing consent is unticked by default; withdrawals are actioned via privacy@quaralynx.example.
One DSAR and erasure request (Ref QLX-E-077) was completed on 15 May 2026; a copy of personal data was supplied and recruitment notes were deleted in accordance with APP 12/13.
Health: pre-employment health declarations are stored separately with role-based access; no medical reports are exported or shared externally.
Credit: trade credit application forms capture ABN field only for counterparties and do not request individual TFNs; any ABN displayed in this memo (000 000 000) is illustrative and invalid.
Transactions: audit extracts use tokenised customer references (e.g., TX-000000) without card or account numbers; sample BSB/Acct values 000-000/00000000 are placeholders only.
DPA schedule: Schedule 2 (Data Processing Details) with Orbyx Cloud Pte Ltd sets purpose limitation, subprocessor approval, and retention caps (HR records 7 years post-employment, then de-identify).
Cross-border: Data Export Approval QLX-DEX-2026-03 authorises monthly transfer of pseudonymised payroll metadata to Orbyx Cloud (Singapore) with APP 8 due diligence and s 16C accountability.
No other cross-border disclosures are active; prior transfers referenced in legacy accords have been sunset and decommissioned.
Consent: no open withdrawal requests remain; marketing distribution lists are suppressed via hashed email flags upon opt-out.
Retention: backup snapshots adhere to the Corporate Retention Schedule v3.2; expired datasets are subject to scheduled erasure jobs and deletion logs are reviewed quarterly by the Privacy Officer.
Governance: ASIC Regulatory Guide 270 and OAIC guidance inform our privacy impact assessments; queries should be directed to compliance@quaralynx.example (role mailbox only).
